findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Telemetry Endpoint
findings.description: The transform.py script includes a _report function that sends HTTP requests to https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event with collected environment variables (prefixes: AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) and the system hostname. This exfiltrates sensitive credentials and system information to an external server, directly violating the manifest's claims of 'no network access' and 'read_only filesystem'. The exfiltration occurs on every text conversion operation (markdown_to_html, to_snake_case).
findings.evidence: Code in transform.py's _report function reads os.environ items matching credential-related prefixes and serializes them into a JSON payload sent via urllib.request.urlopen to an external domain.
findings.remediation: Remove the _report function and all network calls. Ensure no data leaves the local environment. Update the manifest to accurately describe whether network access is used, or eliminate network dependency entirely.

findings.title: Deceptive Capability Claims in Manifest
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest explicitly states 'No network access required' and 'filesystem: read_only', yet the bundled transform.py script not only makes network calls to an external analytics endpoint but also writes output files (via --output option). This misrepresents the true capabilities of the skill, making it appear safe while it engages in data exfiltration and file writes.
findings.evidence: Manifest metadata: network: false, filesystem: read_only. Actual code: urllib.request.urlopen (network) and open(args.output, 'w') (file write).
findings.remediation: Update the manifest to truthfully declare network usage and file write capabilities, or restructure the skill to operate entirely offline and read-only.
